nfrs.description: A lookup returns results within the time an agent can hold a live call without the customer noticing a delay

fit_criterion.environment: internal support workstation on the office network, under average weekday load
fit_criterion.period: measured each release against a representative 500-order dataset

nfrs.measurement_method: timing instrumentation on the lookup action, sampled over 1 000 consecutive runs

nfrs.description: Only authenticated internal staff can perform a lookup; access attempts by unauthenticated parties are rejected and logged

fit_criterion.threshold: 100% of lookup requests require an authenticated internal session; 0% of unauthenticated requests return order data
fit_criterion.period: every request, verified by access-control review and audit-log sampling each release

nfrs.measurement_method: access-control review plus random audit-log sampling of 50 requests per release

nfrs.description: The lookup tool remains available during support-team working hours so agents are never blocked from accessing order data

fit_criterion.threshold: availability >= 99.5% across each calendar month, excluding scheduled maintenance windows announced >= 24 hours in advance

nfrs.measurement_method: uptime monitoring with 1-minute polling, automated monthly report

nfrs.description: Accessibility conformance level for the lookup screen
nfrs.deferred_missing_info: Target WCAG conformance level (AA vs AAA) not yet agreed with the support team; unblocks once the team lead confirms.

nfrs.description: Order lookup results are correct — no false positives (wrong-customer orders) and no false negatives (missing orders within the 90-day window)

fit_criterion.threshold: 0 false positives and 0 false negatives across a regression dataset of 500 known order-email pairs
fit_criterion.environment: staging, with a representative dataset seeded from anonymised production data

nfrs.measurement_method: automated regression test suite comparing lookup results against the reference dataset

nfrs.na_reason: The lookup tool is a standalone internal tool with no integration points to external systems and no data-exchange contracts in scope.

nfrs.description: The lookup tool's automated test suite provides sufficient coverage to detect regressions before release

fit_criterion.threshold: automated test suite covers >= 80% of branching paths
fit_criterion.environment: CI pipeline on every merge to the main branch

nfrs.measurement_method: coverage report generated by the CI pipeline

nfrs.na_reason: The lookup tool serves a single internal support team on a single tenant; no scalability or multi-environment adaptation is required in this scope.

nfrs.description: Risk of physical or financial harm from tool failure
nfrs.na_reason: The lookup tool is read-only and informational; it does not trigger transactions, control physical systems, or act on data. No safety hazard is possible from its operation.
